description: Steel Dynamics is a Fort Wayne-based industrial metals company that produces EAF steel, recycles ferrous and nonferrous scrap, fabricates steel joist and deck systems, and is expanding into aluminum.

overview.title: Business Scope
paragraphs: Steel Dynamics operates across steelmaking, metals recycling, steel fabrication, and newer aluminum operations, giving it a vertically linked model from scrap collection through finished industrial and construction inputs. That makes it less a single steel mill than a circular metals platform, with the usual amount of corporate modesty applied to very heavy machinery.

The company emphasizes electric arc furnace steelmaking and recycled scrap as core inputs. For the registry, the relevant surface is not consumer software lock-in but the transparency of supply chains, product specifications, environmental claims, and procurement dependencies around critical industrial materials.

overview.title: Products And Markets
paragraphs: Flat rolled steel is the primary product family to map first. Steel Dynamics lists hot rolled, cold rolled, coated, painted, floor plate, and related flat roll products serving construction, automotive, manufacturing, transportation, agriculture, energy, and pipe and tube markets.

New Millennium Building Systems is the downstream construction-facing platform, producing steel joists, girders, trusses, deck, and long-span composite floor systems for nonresidential and multistory residential projects. The product category is highly specification-driven, which is exactly where open standards, interoperable design data, and third-party documentation matter.

overview.title: Registry Fit
paragraphs: No technology waves are currently mapped in repo context, and that is reasonable for a first structured sync. The initial registry work should focus on product taxonomy, segment boundaries, evidence URLs, and whether the company exposes enough public product and sustainability documentation to support comparison against lower-carbon, open-standard, or more transparent alternatives.

There is no app store to escape here; the practical question is whether buyers, builders, and manufacturers can compare steel inputs without treating PDFs, proprietary calculators, and marketing-grade sustainability language as sacred tablets.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Official Company Overview
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for business segments, circular manufacturing positioning, operating platforms, and corporate contact details.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Flat Roll Products
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Best source for the Flat Rolled Steel product family, product types, locations, and end markets.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Steel Joist And Decking
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary Steel Dynamics page connecting New Millennium to steel joists, deck, fabrication plants, and construction markets.

sourceSuggestions.title: New Millennium Building Systems
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Product-level source for joists, deck, composite systems, BIM/design tools, catalogs, and the Steel Dynamics subsidiary relationship.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Recycled Metals
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Documents OmniSource, ferrous and nonferrous scrap processing, recycling services, and upstream feedstock relevance.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Circular Manufacturing Model
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful for assessing recycled-input claims, EAF framing, and the company's circularity narrative.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Sustainability Reporting
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Central index for sustainability updates, GSCC certifications, emissions verification, EPD-related materials, and supplementary disclosures.

sourceSuggestions.title: Steel Dynamics Market Capitalization
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Repo-aligned source for current market-cap context and public-company ranking checks.
